3348.en: Sa'id ibn Jubayr once narrated a hadith from the Prophet (peace be upon him), and a man said: 'In the Book of Allah there is something that contradicts this.' He replied: 'I see that I narrate to you from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) and you counter it with the Book of Allah? The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) was more knowledgeable of the Book of Allah, the Exalted, than you are.'

3349.en: Ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'When you are narrated a hadith from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him), assume of it what is most appropriate, most guided, and most God-fearing.'

3350.en: 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'When you are told something from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him), assume of it what is most guided, most God-fearing, and most appropriate.'

3351.en: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: 'Whoever lies about me intentionally, let him take his seat in the Fire.' So Ibn 'Abbas, when he narrated, would say: 'If you hear me narrating from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) and you do not find it in the Book of Allah, nor do the people consider it sound, then know that I have lied about him.'

3352.en: 'Ikrimah said: 'The people least interested in a scholar are his own household.'

3353.en: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Review hadith among yourselves, for hadith stirs up hadith.'

3354.en: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Review hadith among yourselves, for hadith stirs up hadith.'

3355.en: Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Review hadith among yourselves, for hadith stirs up hadith.' [Reported through multiple chains via Abu Bishr, Abu Nadraa, al-Jurayri, and others, all going back to Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, and there is more wording in some versions than this.]

3356.en: Tawus said to 'Amr: 'Come, let us sit with the people.'

3357.en: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Review this hadith and do not let it slip away from you, for it is not like the Quran, which is gathered and preserved. If you do not review this hadith, it will slip away from you. And let none of you say: "I narrated yesterday, so I will not narrate today." Rather, narrate yesterday, and narrate today, and narrate tomorrow.'

3358.en: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Review hadith and recall it, for if you do not recall it, it will go. And let no man say of a hadith he has narrated: "I narrated it once." For whoever heard it gains more knowledge from it, and those who have not heard it will hear it.'

3359.en: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla said: 'Review hadith, for the reviving of hadith is its discussion.'

3360.en: 'Alqamah said: 'Review hadith, for its remembrance is its life.'

3361.en: Ziyad ibn Sa'd said: 'Ibn Shihab used to narrate hadith to the Bedouins.'

3362.en: Al-A'mash said: 'Isma'il ibn Raja' used to gather the children of the school and narrate to them, thereby keeping the hadiths in memory.'

3363.en: Ibrahim said: 'Narrate your hadith to whoever is interested in it and to whoever is not, for it will become with you like an Imam whose text you recite.'

3364.en: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'When you hear hadith from us, review it among yourselves.'

3365.en: Yunus said: 'We used to come to al-Hasan, and when we left his company, we would review it among ourselves.'

3366.en: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'When any of you wishes to narrate a hadith, let him repeat it three times.'

3367.en: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla said: 'The reviving of hadith is its discussion.' 'Abdullah ibn Shaddad said to him: 'May Allah have mercy on you — how many a hadith you have revived in my heart that had died away.'

3368.en: Muhammad ibn Fudayl said from his father: 'Al-Harith ibn Yazid al-'Ukli, Ibn Shubruma, al-Qa'qa' ibn Yazid, and Mughira — when they had prayed the final 'Isha' prayer, they would sit together in matters of fiqh, and nothing would separate them until the adhan for Fajr.'

3369.en: Two of three scholars — 'Ata', Tawus, and Mujahid — said: 'There is no harm in discussing fiqh at night.'

3370.en: Mujahid said: 'There is no harm in discussing fiqh at night.'

3371.en: Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Studying knowledge for an hour of the night is better than spending that hour in prayer.'

3372.en: 'Ata' said: 'We used to come to Jabir ibn 'Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him), and when we left his company we would review it among ourselves. Abu al-Zubayr was the best of us at retaining his hadith.'

3373.en: Marwan narrated: 'Al-Layth ibn Sa'd said: Ibn Shihab reviewed hadith one night after 'Isha', remaining in that sitting, occupied with the discussion of hadith, until morning.'

3374.en: Al-Zuhri said: 'Whenever I met 'Ubayd Allah ibn 'Abdullah, it was as if I were drawing water from a vast ocean.'

3375.en: 'Uthman ibn 'Abdullah said: 'Al-Harith al-'Ukli and his companions used to sit together at night and discuss fiqh.'

3376.en: 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Review this hadith, for its life is its discussion.'

3377.en: 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) asked his companions when they came to visit him: 'Do you sit together?' They said: 'We do not give that up.' He asked: 'And do you visit one another?' They said: 'Yes, O Abu 'Abd al-Rahman — a man among us may miss his brother and walk to the farthest part of Kufa to find him.' He said: 'You will continue to be in good as long as you do that.'

3378.en: Al-Zuhri said: 'The bane of knowledge is forgetfulness and abandoning discussion.'

3379.en: 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'The bane of hadith is forgetfulness.'

3380.en: 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: 'Everything has a bane, and the bane of knowledge is forgetfulness.'

3381.en: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: 'The bane of knowledge is forgetfulness, and its destruction is to narrate it to those unworthy of it.'

3382.en: Al-Hasan said: 'The affliction of knowledge is forgetfulness.'

3383.en: 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Review this hadith and visit one another, for if you do not, it will be erased.'

3384.en: Al-Zuhri said: 'I used to think that I had acquired knowledge, then I sat with 'Ubayd Allah [ibn 'Abdullah], and it was as though I had been in a mountain pass.'

3385.en: It was said to 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz: 'If only you would unite the people upon one thing.' He replied: 'I would not be pleased if they had not differed.' Then he wrote to the various regions and cities: 'Let each people judge by what their jurists have agreed upon.'

3386.en: 'Awn ibn 'Abdullah said: 'I would not like for the Companions of the Prophet (peace be upon him) not to have differed. For had they agreed on one thing and a man abandoned it, he would have abandoned the Sunnah. But because they differed, if a man follows the view of any one of them, he has followed the Sunnah.'

3387.en: Tawus said: 'Ibn 'Abbas would sometimes form an opinion and then abandon it.'

3388.en: Marwan ibn al-Hakam narrated: 'Uthman ibn 'Affan said to me: 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said to me: I have formed an opinion regarding the grandfather [in inheritance]. Follow it if you see fit; and if you follow the view of the elder before me, he was a fine man of opinion.' [And Abu Bakr treated the grandfather as a father.]

3389.en: 'Asim al-Ahwal said: 'I presented the hadith of fiqh to al-Sha'bi and he approved them for me.'

3390.en: Sufyan ibn 'Uyayna said: 'I asked 'Amr ibn Dinar: Did you hear Jabir ibn 'Abdullah say that the Prophet (peace be upon him) told a man passing through the mosque with arrows: "Hold them by their heads"?' He said: 'Yes.'

3391.en: Sufyan said: 'I asked 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Qasim: Did you hear your father narrating from 'A'isha that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) used to kiss her while fasting?' He said: 'Yes.'

3392.en: Shu'bah said: 'Mansur wrote to me with a hadith, and when I met him I said: "May I narrate it from you?" He replied: "Is it not the case that when I wrote to you, I narrated to you?"' He also asked Ayyub al-Sakhtiyani, who said the same.

3393.en: 'Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak said from Ma'mar from al-Zuhri: 'I presented a book to him and said: "May I narrate it from you?" He replied: "Who else narrated it to you?"'

3394.en: Hisham ibn 'Urwa said from his father: 'Presenting a book [for approval] and direct oral transmission are equivalent.'

3395.en: Ja'far ibn Muhammad said from his father: 'Presenting a book and direct oral transmission are equivalent.'

3396.en: Dawud ibn 'Ata' said: 'Zayd ibn Aslam considered presenting a book equivalent to direct oral transmission, and Ibn Abi Dhi'b held the same view.'

3397.en: Malik ibn Anas considered presentation and oral transmission to be equivalent.

3398.en: Al-A'mash said: 'Ibrahim used to say he [a person standing in prayer] should stand to his left.' Then al-A'mash was narrated a report from Sami' al-Zayyat from Ibn 'Abbas that the Prophet (peace be upon him) positioned him to his right, and Ibrahim accepted that and acted by it.

3399.en: Al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba (may Allah be pleased with him) narrated: 'Umar sought people's testimony [asking]: "Has anyone heard from the Prophet (peace be upon him) [regarding the blood money for a fetus]?" The one against whom judgment was made stood up and said: "The Prophet (peace be upon him) judged against me that I owe a ghurra — a male or female slave." I said: "Do you judge against me in a matter for which it did not eat, or drink, or cry at birth, or speak? It should be left uncompensated — that is what is most fitting to be left." The Prophet (peace be upon him) moved toward him with something in his hand and said: "Is this poetry?" 'Umar said: "Had not the judgment of the Prophet (peace be upon him) reached me, I would have made it differently."'

3400.en: Sa'id ibn 'Amir narrated that Sallam mentioned from Ayyub who said: 'If you wish to know the mistakes of your teacher, sit with someone other than him.'

3401.en: Ayyub said: 'We were discussing in Makkah the case of a woman whose husband dies: I said her waiting period begins from when the news reaches her, based on what al-Hasan, Qatadah, and our companions said. Then Talq ibn Habib al-'Anazi met me and said: [that the scholars say] from the day he died.' He said: 'I asked Muhammad ibn Sirin, who said: from the day he died. And Nafi' narrated to me that Ibn 'Umar said: from the day he died. And I heard 'Ikrima say: from the day he died. And Jabir ibn Zayd said: from the day he died. And Ibn 'Abbas said: from the day he died. And Hammad reported that he heard Layth narrate from al-Hakam that 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: from the day he died.'

3402.en: Al-Hakam ibn Mas'ud said: 'We came to 'Umar in the case of the musharraka [the shared inheritance case] and he did not share [the full blood brothers in the estate]. We came back the following year and he did share them. We said to him [about the change]: He replied: "That was how we judged then, and this is how we judge now."'

3403.en: Ibn Munabbih said: 'The scholars of the past used to guard their knowledge from the people of worldly affairs, so the people of worldly affairs were eager for their knowledge and would offer their worldly wealth for it. But the scholars of today have offered their knowledge to the people of worldly affairs, so the people of worldly affairs have become indifferent to their knowledge, and stingy with their worldly wealth toward them.'

3404.en: Al-Dahhak ibn Musa narrated: 'Sulayman ibn 'Abd al-Malik passed through Madinah while heading to Makkah and stayed there for some days. He asked: "Is there among the people of this city a man who encountered the companions of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him)?" He was directed to Abu Hazim. He called for him and conversed with him at length. Then Sulayman said: "Give me advice." Abu Hazim said: "I will advise you briefly: Exalt your Lord and sanctify Him from seeing you where He has forbidden you, or finding you absent where He has commanded you." When Abu Hazim left, Sulayman sent after him a sum of money. Abu Hazim sent it back, saying: "I have no need of it."' [The full account contains more detail.]

3405.en: A scholar said: 'O seeker of knowledge, act upon your knowledge, give away surplus wealth, and withhold excess speech except as a duty from the Right. O seeker of knowledge, do not let what belongs to another distract you from what belongs to you. O seeker of knowledge, sit with the scholars, press close to them, listen to them, and leave off disputing with them. O seeker of knowledge, honor the scholars for their knowledge and belittle the ignorant for their ignorance — but do not distance yourself from them; draw them near and teach them. O seeker of knowledge, do not narrate a hadith in a gathering until you understand it, and do not answer a man in what he says until you know what he has said to you. O seeker of knowledge, exalt your Lord.'

3406.en: 'Abbad ibn 'Abbad al-Khawwas al-Shami said: 'Use your reason — and reason is a blessing — though many a person of sound mind has occupied his heart with deep speculation about what is harmful to him. [The scholar-preachers] loved the world and disliked the station of those given to it, so they shared their way of living while distancing themselves from them in speech, defending themselves by words from being attributed to their deeds. They neither freed themselves from what they rejected nor entered into what they attributed to themselves — for the one who acts by the truth speaks even when silent. And it has been mentioned that Allah, the Exalted, says [addressing such scholars]: I am angry with you above my anger at the ignorant, for you know and act otherwise.'
